feature.name: Username and Password Login
feature.description:
As an existing customer
  I want to log in using my username and password
  So that I can access my account securely from any device

scenarios.name: Successful login navigates to home screen

steps.text: I enter a valid username
I enter the correct password
I tap 'Sign in'
an inline 'Login successful!' message is shown
I am automatically navigated to the app home screen within approximately 1 second
the failed attempt counter for my username is cleared

scenarios.name: Migrated user logs in with non-email username

steps.text: I enter my existing The Bank username in non-email format
I enter the correct password
I tap 'Sign in'
authentication succeeds
I am navigated to the home screen
I enter valid credentials and tap 'Sign in'
the button label changes to 'Signing in...'
the button is disabled to prevent duplicate submissions
a loading state is shown while authentication is in progress
I tap 'Sign in' with either field empty
I see: 'Please enter both username and password.'
the button returns to its active state

scenarios.name: Invalid credentials show error without disclosing attempt count

steps.text: I enter an incorrect username and password combination
I tap 'Sign in'
I see: 'Invalid username or password. Please try again.'
no information about remaining attempts is shown to the user

scenarios.name: Account locked after 3 consecutive failed attempts

steps.text: I have failed login 2 times with this username
I enter incorrect credentials and tap 'Sign in' again
I see: 'Your account has been locked due to multiple failed login attempts. Please contact customer support to unlock your account.'
the account is locked for 30 minutes server-side

scenarios.name: Account suspended after 3 consecutive lockout cycles

steps.text: my account has been locked 3 times consecutively
I attempt to log in again after the lockout period
my account is suspended
I must contact the Contact Centre for manual re-enablement

scenarios.name: Network error during authentication allows retry

steps.text: a network error occurs during authentication
an error message is shown
I can retry by tapping 'Sign in' again

scenarios.name: Session expires while login flow is active

steps.text: my session times out while I am on the login screen
I am redirected to the login screen
any unsaved progress is lost

scenarios.name: Quick login expired banner shown when arriving from expired session

steps.text: I arrived at this screen from a 'Quick Login Expired' state
an amber banner is shown: 'Quick Login Expired. For your security, please sign in again with your email and password.'

scenarios.name: Biometric shortcut preserves partially entered form data

steps.text: I have partially entered my username
I tap the biometric login shortcut button
the biometric flow is initiated
the partially entered form data is preserved if I return
authentication succeeds
the transition from login screen to home screen is direct
no separate full-screen success page is shown

scenarios.name: Rapid screen taps during success state do not cause duplicate navigation

steps.text: the 'Login successful!' message is shown
I rapidly tap the screen multiple times
no duplicate navigation events are triggered

scenarios.name: Password field is always masked — no show/hide toggle

steps.text: I am on the login screen
the password field is masked at all times
no show/hide toggle is shown
I am on the username and password login screen
both the username and password fields are empty

endpoints.description: Authenticate a user with username and password. On success returns a session token, clears the failed-attempt counter, and the client navigates to the home screen. On failure the failed-attempt counter is incremented; after the 3rd consecutive failure the account is locked for 30 minutes (423), and after 3 consecutive lockout cycles the account is suspended (403).

Clear the failed-attempt counter for a username. Invoked automatically by the backend on a successful POST /api/v1/auth/login (see happy-path scenario 'the failed attempt counter for my username is cleared'). Exposed as a separate endpoint so that administrative unlock flows can also reset the counter.

responses.description: Login succeeded. Body contains the session token, user id, and expiry. Client clears local form state and navigates to the home screen.
Validation error (e.g. empty username or password). Client displays the inline 'Please enter both username and password.' message and keeps the user on the login screen.
Invalid credentials. Client displays 'Invalid username or password. Please try again.' and MUST NOT disclose remaining attempts or the account's lockout state to the user.
Account is locked (server-side 30-minute lockout triggered by 3 consecutive failed attempts). Client displays the locked message and prompts the user to contact customer support.
Account is suspended (3 consecutive lockout cycles). Client displays the suspension message and directs the user to the Contact Centre for manual re-enablement.
Server / network error during authentication. Client shows a generic error message and allows the user to retry by tapping 'Sign in' again.

strings.value: Please enter both username and password.
Invalid username or password. Please try again.
Your account has been locked due to multiple failed login attempts. Please contact customer support to unlock your account.
Quick Login Expired. For your security, please sign in again with your email and password.

transitions.trigger: tap 'Sign in' with both username and password non-empty
server-side session timeout while still on the login screen

assumptions: The login endpoint is POST /api/v1/auth/login with JSON body { username, password }. The feature does not specify the path or method, so this is an inferred RESTful convention.
The 'app home screen' lives at the /home route. The feature never names the route.
The biometric login shortcut is a separate route (/login/biometric), inferred from the parallel feature file 1594_biometric_login.feature and the step text 'the biometric flow is initiated'.
The 'failed attempt counter' is a server-side per-username counter, cleared automatically on a successful login. The 30-minute lockout is a server-side state with a TTL.
Account suspension after 3 consecutive lockout cycles is a server-side state change. The client simply displays the suspension message and Contact Centre guidance.
The inline 'Login successful!' message is a transient toast/notification, not a full-screen page (per the 'No full-screen success page shown' scenario).
The 'Signing in...' loading state is reflected on the button itself (label change + disabled state) — not a separate spinner overlay.
The amber 'Quick Login Expired' banner is a UI overlay above the form, conditionally rendered when the quickLoginExpired flag is true on screen entry.
Password masking is achieved with a type='password' input. The feature explicitly forbids a show/hide toggle, so the generated component must not render one.
The 'approximately 1 second' navigation timing is informational, not a hard SLA — the generated tests should not assert an exact timeout but rather wait for the home screen to appear.
Rapid-tap deduplication is achieved by disabling the Sign in button during the success transition (not by debouncing the navigation route itself).
Session expiry while on the login screen does not lose any user-entered data because the login form state is local component state, not part of the server session.
